title: navarretia linearifolia subsp. pinnatisecta
intro: Navarretia linearifolia subsp. pinnatisecta, also known as pinnate-lobed navarretia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family, native to California, USA. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Navarretia linearifolia subsp. pinnatisecta include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol to extract its medicinal compounds.
